description:
You are the site administrator and need a quick per-user overview of recent SSH login activity.

1. There is a log file already present at  
   /home/user/logs/auth.log

2. From this file you must create a summary report named  
   /home/user/output/login_summary.txt

3. The report must contain exactly one line per user that appears in the log, sorted alphabetically by the user name (case-sensitive, lowercase letters in this dataset).

4. Each line must follow this *precise* format (no extra spaces, tabs or punctuation):

   username:SUCCESS=<number>,FAIL=<number>

   • username – the account name that appears in the log.  
   • <number> – an integer count of how many times that user had the indicated result.

5. Count rules  
   • A log line that contains the text “Accepted password for <user>” is a SUCCESS for that user.  
   • A log line that contains the text “Failed password for <user>”   is a FAIL    for that user.  
   • Ignore all other lines (there are none in this simplified log, but write the commands so they do not break if more appear).  

6. When you are finished, the file /home/user/output/login_summary.txt must exist and have the exact contents described above so that an automated test can read and verify it.

Hint: you should only need 2-3 shell commands (for example, a pipeline using grep/awk/sort or similar) to build the summary.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):

Directory structure  
/home/user/logs/              – exists, permissions 755  
/home/user/output/            – does NOT exist yet

File /home/user/logs/auth.log (permissions 644) with the following exact eight lines:

Jan 10 10:00:00 localhost sshd[12345]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.5 port 52222 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:05 localhost sshd[12346]: Failed password for bob from 192.168.1.6 port 52223 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:10 localhost sshd[12347]: Failed password for alice from 192.168.1.7 port 52224 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:15 localhost sshd[12348]: Accepted password for carol from 192.168.1.8 port 52225 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:20 localhost sshd[12349]: Failed password for carol from 192.168.1.9 port 52226 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:25 localhost sshd[12350]: Accepted password for bob from 192.168.1.10 port 52227 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:30 localhost sshd[12351]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.11 port 52228 ssh2  
Jan 10 10:00:35 localhost sshd[12352]: Failed password for dave from 192.168.1.12 port 52229 ssh2  

Expected final state (after the agent completes):

/home/user/output/ directory now exists, permissions 755 (default mkdir)  

File /home/user/output/login_summary.txt (permissions 644) exists and contains exactly these four newline-terminated lines in this order:

alice:SUCCESS=2,FAIL=1  
bob:SUCCESS=1,FAIL=1  
carol:SUCCESS=1,FAIL=1  
dave:SUCCESS=0,FAIL=1
name: task_000000_86122c4a
